commands.description: Ensure raw input is never concatenated into SQL strings. Use parameterized queries/prepared statements.
commands.mistake: Using template literals: `SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${req.query.id}`, which exposes database schemas directly to injection payloads.

commands.description: Escape all untrusted data before rendering it in the HTML DOM, or use libraries that do it automatically.
commands.mistake: Using dangerouslySetInnerHTML or innerHTML directly on raw inputs from search query parameters or user profiles.

commands.description: Insecure Direct Object Reference / Broken Object Level Authorization. Never trust user-provided IDs without verifying ownership.
commands.mistake: Relying on client-side route guards or URL obfuscation to protect access to private files or sensitive data records.

commands.description: Sanitize and validate user-supplied URLs against an allowlist before making requests from the backend server.
commands.mistake: Making raw HTTP fetch calls to user-submitted URLs directly, enabling attackers to probe internal loopback services like localhost/metadata.

commands.description: Ensure JWT tokens are verified using robust cryptography keys and expiration checks on the server.
commands.mistake: Using jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify(), which extracts header payload fields without checking if the token signature is valid.

commands.description: Restricts resources (such as JavaScript, CSS, Images) that the browser is allowed to load for a given page.
commands.mistake: Using 'unsafe-inline' or '*' in CSP directives, which completely defeats the anti-XSS features of CSP.

commands.description: Forces browser connections to happen securely over HTTPS instead of HTTP.
commands.mistake: Enabling HSTS with a short max-age duration or without testing subdomains, potentially locking out users from legacy HTTP APIs.

commands.description: Prevents clickjacking attacks by dictating whether pages can be embedded within frame tags on external websites.
commands.mistake: Neglecting to declare X-Frame-Options (or using CSP frame-ancestors), enabling bad actors to overlay transparent clickable layers on your page.

commands.description: Forces the browser to respect content-type headers, preventing MIME-type sniffing vulnerabilities.
commands.mistake: Forgetting to set 'nosniff', which allows browsers to execute uploaded user text/HTML assets as scripts.

commands.description: Controls how much reference metadata (such as paths or search queries) is sent when navigating to external websites.
commands.mistake: Using Referrer-Policy 'unsafe-url', which leaks sensitive query tokens (e.g. password-reset links) to external analytics scripts.

commands.description: Never store plain text passwords. Use standard slow hashing functions like bcrypt or Argon2 with high cost factors.
commands.example:
const hash = await bcrypt.hash(rawPassword, 12);
const matches = await bcrypt.compare(inputPassword, hash);
commands.mistake: Using fast hashing algorithms like MD5, SHA-1, or SHA-256 for passwords, which are easily cracked via dictionary attacks or GPUs.

commands.description: Use Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) with Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) for authenticated encryption/decryption of private text.
commands.mistake: Reusing Initialization Vectors (IVs) or using insecure encryption modes like AES-ECB, which leak patterns in encrypted data.

commands.description: Use anti-CSRF tokens or enforce strict SameSite attributes on cookies to prevent cross-site request forgery attacks.
commands.mistake: Leaving SameSite as 'None' without adding CSRF tokens, allowing cross-site clicks to make state-changing GET/POST requests silently.

commands.description: Always use cryptographically secure pseudorandom number generators (CSPRNG) for sensitive keys, IDs, or tokens.
commands.mistake: Using Math.random() to generate session IDs, passwords, or salts, which are mathematically predictable and easily reverse-engineered.

commands.description: Limit the number of requests a client can make in a given timeframe to prevent DoS attacks and brute-force logins.
commands.mistake: Rate-limiting using local server memory in stateless cluster environments, which allows clients to bypass limits by hit-testing different instances.

commands.description: Restrict client-side cross-origin access to your APIs. Maintain a strict allowlist of origins instead of allowing any domain.
commands.mistake: Setting 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' combined with 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true', which is an invalid and highly insecure CORS policy.
